privileged_info_summary: The add_admin_flight_count task references a real Django application (Aerobridge) and real model (Aircraft from registry app) with authentic fixture data (from openutm-labs/aerobridge open-source project). The target file /opt/aerobridge/registry/admin.py exists in the real codebase. However, there is a critical structural contradiction: the task asks to count 'Flight Plans associated with each aircraft,' but the FlightPlan model has NO relationship to Aircraft in the actual codebase. FlightPlan contains only JSON fields and timestamps. The actual relationship exists between Aircraft and FlightOperation (via drone field), not FlightPlan. Additionally, the setup_task.sh script attempts to create FlightPlan objects with an 'aircraft' parameter that does not exist in the model definition, which would cause a runtime error. The Aerobridge repository is archived (December 2023), and Aircraft is registered with SimpleHistoryAdmin (no custom AircraftAdmin class exists in the real codebase).
